medical_history.history_of_present_illness: ESRD secondary to FSGS with renal transplant, which failed immediately s/p bilateral nephrectomy and nephrectom of transplanted kidney, peritoneal dialysis until patient presented with ascites and encapsulating peritoneal sclerosis. Symptoms included malaise and lightheadedness at home for ___ days, associated with some diarrhea. Patient presented to the ED after feeling pre-syncopal. Found to be septic with fever to 102, tachycardic to 120s, hypotensive to 70/40s, and abdomen diffusely tender. Lactate was 3.5. Received 1L of crystalloid, IV vancomycin, and Zosyn. CT scan revealed intrabdominal free air and increased ascites.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Pituitary mass, history of thyroid cancer, FSGS, end stage renal disease, history of renal transplant, encapsulating peritoneal sclerosis, pancreatitis, stroke, hyperprolactinemia, hypogonadism, EOE/GERD, lactose intolerance, constipation/dysmotility

physical_examination.general_examination: Vitals: T 102.0, HR 127, BP 111/71, RR 18, Sat 97% RA. Patient was uncomfortable, lethargic, and oriented x 3. Abdomen was distended, diffusely tender with guarding. Extremities were warm to the touch, no peripheral edema.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurological: Not specified. Cardiovascular: Tachycardic. Other: Abdomen soft, appropriately tender, and nondistended.
